mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >60% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during periods of rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer spending and construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, with positive outcomes occurring in >65% of co-occurring instances, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals strengthening economic momentum and rising corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative forward returns in >60% of cases during early-cycle phases, or the predictive relationship reverses sign when inflation exceeds 3.0 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and robust consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates with negative 20-day forward returns in >58% of observations, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.015 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 210, elevated inflation erodes real corporate profits and forces tighter monetary policy expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 210 coincide with positive forward returns in >60% of cases, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for nominal interest rate levels.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and real personal income confirms broad-based economic strength and rising household purchasing power, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined growth signal fails to predict positive returns, with negative outcomes dominating (>55%) when both series expand simultaneously, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating industrial production growth signals weakening manufacturing demand and impending earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration predicts positive forward returns in >60% of late-cycle observations, or the signal loses statistical significance when housing market indicators remain stable.
